entry.title: Bitcoin covenants are inevitable
entry.summary: The discussion revolves around the implications of discontinuing the block reward in Bitcoin's economic model. The argument presented suggests that if the block reward were to be maintained indefinitely, perhaps through an inflationary hard fork, the demand for block space might not play a crucial role in securing Bitcoin. This perspective challenges the conventional understanding that transaction fees will become the primary security budget after the last Bitcoin is mined and the block rewards cease. For further details on these insights, Peter Todd provides more extensive coverage on this topic, which can be accessed [here](https://petertodd.org).
